criteriaCoverage.criterion: The builder renders an existing definition as a downward tree with branches fanning sideways + rounded orthogonal connectors; no diagonal/upward lines; layout is auto-computed (no manual drag). Verified in a REAL browser (Playwright).
criteriaCoverage.evidence: VERIFIED IN CHROMIUM: automation-builder.browser.e2e.spec.ts test 'renders a seeded definition as a downward tree with axis-aligned connectors' passed (1.4s). It opens the seeded 'Welcome journey' branching automation, asserts node-trigger/wait/condition/send/exit visible, reads every automation-connectors <path d> in-page and runs pathIsAxisAligned() (rejects any L/Q segment that moves both axes) — all axis-aligned; asserts waitTop>triggerTop and condTop>waitTop (down-only via DOM boundingBox); asserts the two exits sit in distinct x columns (Set(lefts).size>1, fanned sideways). Layout is fully computed: web/src/automations/layout.ts derives depth (BFS longest-path) and col (subtree-width packing) from edges ONLY — input x/y ignored; computeEdges THROWS if toPoint.y<=fromPoint.y. orthogonalPath emits only M/V/H + clamped Q corners and throws when the target is not below the source. Unit tier: layout.test.ts (9) + orthogonal-path.test.ts (5) all pass; no stored coordinates anywhere (grep confirmed).

criteriaCoverage.criterion: A user can assemble trigger → wait → send → exit AND an if-branch (yes/no each ending in a send→exit), save it, reload, and get the same graph back (round-trips through the DSL). Saving an invalid graph is refused.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: VERIFIED IN CHROMIUM: e2e 'assemble trigger→wait→send→exit via the (+) palette, then save' (3.0s), 'assemble an if-branch → two arms fan into distinct x columns' (2.3s), and 'round-trip: save then reopen reconstructs the same graph' (2.8s) all passed — reopen via automation-open rebuilds the same node set (trigger/wait/exit count 1). DSL round-trip is exercised against REAL Postgres in services/local-api/test/get-automation.integration.test.ts (5/5): POST then GET /automations/:id returns definition deep-equal to the input for both linear and branching defs, and the returned branch def passes the real validateAutomationDefinition. Invalid-graph refusal: that integration test posts cyclic/no-exit/orphan/two-trigger defs and asserts each is refused with the validator's message surfaced (cycle/orphan/exit/one-trigger); PUT of an invalid def is refused and re-GET shows the prior definition unchanged. Web unit model.test.ts (15) proves buildDefinition(parseDefinition(def)) === def for linear + branch.

criteriaCoverage.criterion: The editor cannot create a loop, a back-edge, or an orphan node; deleting a node re-links to a valid tree. Workspace-scoped; data-testid contract preserved + extended.
criteriaCoverage.evidence: Structurally enforced in mutate.ts: insertOnEdge ONLY rewrites A→B into A→NEW→B (condition auto-wires onTrue→B + onFalse→fresh exit) so a back-edge is unconstructable; deleteNode splices a single-out node or removes a condition + its onFalse-exclusive descendants, and THROWS MutationError on deleting the trigger or removing the last reachable exit, plus a dangling-edge guard. EVERY result is re-checked by the production validateAutomationDefinition. mutate.test.ts (9) includes a property test: 60 random insert/delete steps, the model validates after each step and refusals are always MutationError. VERIFIED IN CHROMIUM: e2e 'delete a node re-links the graph and stays valid' (2.4s) — delete via ActionMenu(node-delete)+app-dialog confirm (NOT window.confirm), trigger re-links to exit, no orphan, save succeeds; and 'deleting the last exit is refused with a styled toast' (1.2s) — styled toast with /exit/i, exit remains. Workspace-scoped: getAutomation uses scopedQuery(ctx.workspaceId,...) which mechanically prepends workspace_id=$1; cross-workspace GET 404s (integration test). data-testid contract preserved + extended (automation-builder/name/list/item/canvas/connectors/edge-insert/palette, node-<type>, node-delete, palette-* x8, design-email).

result.notes: idempotencyOk/webhookSafetyOk = true as NOT-APPLICABLE to phase 5: this phase adds only the builder UI + GET /automations/:id; no runner tick, no outbox/dispatcher send, and no webhook execution are touched here (those are the runner's existing guarantees and phase 6's per-node editors). Verification performed cold: pnpm typecheck --force (43/43, 0 cached); DATABASE_URL=...:5433/cdp pnpm test = web unit 94 pass/4 skip + local-api integration 194 pass (incl. get-automation 5/5 vs REAL Postgres) + acceptance/tests 45 pass, no regression; pnpm lint = exactly the 7 known pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 'rule not found' errors in untouched files (EmailDesigner/Activity/ProfileDetail/SegmentBuilder/SimpleScreens) — ZERO new lint errors in the phase files; web automation unit 38/38 importing the REAL validateAutomationDefinition (no mock). REAL BROWSER: pnpm --filter @cdp/web test:e2e full suite = 77 passed / 1 flaky (unrelated company-ses-config, passed on retry); the automation-builder spec re-run standalone in Chromium = 7/7 passed (17.8s). Version bumped 0.32.0→0.33.0; CLAUDE.md updated with an accurate automation-canvas section + phase-6 deferral. No native dialogs (askConfirm/showToast only), save Button wired onClick={save} (kit auto-lock), no stored coordinates.
recommendations: Invalid-graph saves return HTTP 500 (the validator throws and dispatch maps it to 500) rather than a 400/409. The validator's message is surfaced and the row is not mutated, so it is functionally a clean refusal, but a 4xx with a typed error would be more correct and lets the SPA distinguish a user error from a server fault. Consider catching validateAutomationDefinition in create/updateAutomation and returning ok({error}, 400).

defaultNodeConfig('send') seeds template_id:'placeholder' which validates structurally but is not a real cross-workspace-checked template. Phase 6's send-node attach must run the clone-into-copy + validateSenderId/template ownership path before the automation can be activated/sent (already noted as deferred in CLAUDE.md).
The 1 flaky e2e (company-ses-config 'element is not stable') is pre-existing and unrelated to this phase but recurs under full-suite load; worth stabilizing separately (it passed on retry).
